name: HeroMedia
usage_hint: Full-bleed background media layer for hero sections. Supports static image, carousel (fade/slide), and video modes with parallax, ken-burns, and zoom-on-scroll effects. Positioned absolute to fill its parent section container.
when_to_use: As the background media layer inside any hero section with position:relative and overflow:hidden. Use for landing page heroes, campaign headers, collection banners, and any section needing immersive full-bleed background media.
when_not_to_use: Not for inline product images (use ProductGallery). Not for thumbnail galleries (use ProductHero). Not for standalone video players (use VideoPlayer). Not for card-based carousels (use MediaCarousel).
anti_patterns: Using without a parent that has position:relative and overflow:hidden (media escapes bounds)
Setting parallax effect on mobile (janky on iOS Safari due to scroll handling)
Using carousel with interval < 3000ms (disorienting, hurts readability of overlaid text)
Using video without poster (shows black frame on slow connections)
Combining ken-burns effect with carousel mode (conflicting animations)
Setting overlay to 0 when text is overlaid on a busy image (text becomes unreadable)

examples.label: Static hero image with parallax and dark overlay
Carousel with fade transition and autoplay

effect.description: Scroll/animation effect applied to the media. Only for image mode.

slides.description: Array of slide objects for carousel mode

videoSrc.description: Video URL for video mode (MP4 recommended)

poster.description: Poster image shown before video loads

muted.description: Must be true for autoplay to work in most browsers

overlay.description: Overlay opacity as percentage (0-100). Use 40-70 for text readability over busy images.

overlayColor.description: Overlay color (hex). Overridden by --lx-hero-overlay-color CSS variable.

minHeight.description: Minimum height of the media container. Overridden by --lx-hero-min-height CSS variable.

loading.description: Image loading strategy. Use eager for above-fold heroes.

internals.note: All image and video rendering is delegated to the MediaLoop primitive (handles srcSet, webp, shimmer, video auto-detection, objectFit). HeroMedia adds hero-specific orchestration: parallax, ken-burns, zoom-on-scroll, carousel state machine, overlay, and full-bleed positioning.
